questions.text: What is the historical context of Surah Al-Kafirun?
questions.answer: The leaders of the Quraysh proposed a compromise: they would worship Allah for a year if the Prophet worshipped their idols for a year.

questions.text: What is the Prophet commanded to say in the first verse?
questions.answer: 'O disbelievers' (Ya ayyuhal kafirun).

questions.text: What is the main declaration regarding worship in Verse 2?
questions.answer: 'I do not worship what you worship'.

questions.text: Does the Prophet say 'nor are you worshippers of what I worship'?
questions.answer: Yes, twice (in Verses 3 and 5).

questions.text: What is the final verse of the Surah?
questions.answer: 'For you is your religion, and for me is my religion' (Lakum dinukum waliya din).

questions.text: Why is the statement about worship repeated?
questions.answer: To emphasize a total and absolute refusal of compromise in matters of faith (Tawhid).

questions.text: Does this Surah imply that all religions are equal?
questions.answer: No, it highlights an irreconcilable difference between monotheism and polytheism.

questions.text: What is the virtue of reciting this Surah before sleeping?
questions.answer: It is a 'disavowal of Shirk' (associating partners with Allah).

questions.text: Is this a Makkan or Madinan Surah?
questions.answer: Makkan.

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah Al-Kafirun?
questions.answer: Six verses.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Kafirun' mean?
questions.answer: The Disbelievers.

questions.text: Does 'Lakum dinukum' mean the Prophet accepted their idols?
questions.answer: No, it means a final separation and that they will face the consequences of their false religion.

questions.text: What name is this Surah also known by?
questions.answer: Al-Ikhlas (Sincerity) or Al-Mukaqshishah (The Remover of Disease).

questions.text: What did the Prophet (SAW) frequently recite in the sunnah of Fajr and Maghrib?
questions.answer: Surah Al-Kafirun and Surah Al-Ikhlas.

questions.text: What is 'Ma a'bud' referring to in the Surah?
questions.answer: The object of worship (Allah).

questions.text: What is 'Abid' referring to?
questions.answer: The worshipper.

questions.text: Why does the Surah start with 'Qul' (Say)?
questions.answer: To show it is a direct command from Allah that the Prophet must convey exactly.

questions.text: What is the spiritual significance of the Surah today?
questions.answer: It teaches Muslims to be firm in their faith and not compromise core beliefs for social or political pressure.

questions.text: Does the Surah address the 'Kafirun' with hatred or with a clear boundary of truth?
questions.answer: With a clear and firm boundary of truth.

questions.text: What is the final word of the Surah?
questions.answer: Din (Religion / Way of life).
